description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a double set of straight, parallel wings supported by struts and wires, painted in a light green color with roundel insignias on the wingtips and tail. The fuselage is streamlined and features a wooden texture at the nose and tail, with a cockpit situated at the front of the aircraft. Two large, wooden propellers are mounted at the front, indicating a twin-engine configuration. The tail section includes a horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, both with matching roundel markings. The landing gear appears fixed and consists of two main wheels and a tail skid. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military reconnaissance or bomber aircraft.
description: A vintage green biplane with twin wooden propellers, double wings, and roundel insignias, featuring a front cockpit and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin wooden propellers, double wings, and the cockpit at the nose.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the wing structure, tail section, and roundel insignias on the wingtips and tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the cockpit and fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view emphasizing the wing struts, tail, and the fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings supported by vertical struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines with wooden propellers mounted at the front of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located at the front of the fuselage, likely single or tandem seat.
